title: lithophila subscaposa
intro: Lithophila subscaposa, also known as the rock moss or lithophila, is a low-growing, creeping, or tufted terrestrial plant species within the family Crassulaceae. The plant has been utilized in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Lithophila subscaposa is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. It is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and in some cultures, the plant is consumed raw or dried for its medicinal purposes.
